title: Vishu
intro: Vishu is one of the most important Hindu festivals celebrated in the Indian state of Kerala.
description:
Vishu is one of the most important Hindu festivals celebrated in the Indian state of Kerala. It marks the traditional Malayalam New Year and symbolizes prosperity, abundance, and new beginnings. Vishu is observed every year on 14 or 15 April, when the Sun enters Mesha (Aries) according to the Hindu solar calendar. The festival is considered highly auspicious and is celebrated with prayers, family gatherings, and traditional customs.

One of the most significant traditions of Vishu is the Vishukkani, the first auspicious sight seen at dawn on Vishu day. The Vishukkani is a carefully arranged display that includes an idol or image of Lord Krishna, rice, fruits, vegetables, coconut, flowers, a mirror, lamps, gold ornaments, and other auspicious items. Devotees believe that seeing the Vishukkani first thing in the morning brings prosperity, happiness, and success throughout the year.

On Vishu, devotees visit temples, worship Lord Krishna, prepare festive meals, and celebrate with their families. Elders traditionally give Vishu Kaineettam (money or gifts) to children and younger family members as a blessing for a prosperous year. The festival promotes gratitude, optimism, family unity, and faith in divine blessings.

Festival Month

Mesha Sankranti (April)

Significance / Importance

Vishu holds immense religious, cultural, and spiritual significance in Kerala. The festival marks the beginning of a new solar year and symbolizes prosperity, success, and divine blessings. Devotees worship Lord Krishna and pray for peace, happiness, good health, and abundance in the coming year.

Vishu also strengthens family bonds through traditions such as Vishukkani and Vishu Kaineettam. The festival encourages gratitude, positive thinking, generosity, and hope while reminding devotees to begin the new year with faith and devotion.

Puja Vidhi

1. Wake up early before sunrise.
2. View the Vishukkani as the first sight of the day.
3. Take a holy bath and wear clean or new traditional clothes.
4. Worship Lord Krishna with flowers, fruits, incense, lamps, and offerings.
5. Chant Krishna Mantras or recite the Vishnu Sahasranama.
6. Perform Aarti and offer Prasad.
7. Elders give Vishu Kaineettam (money or gifts) to younger family members.
8. Pray for prosperity, happiness, good health, and success throughout the year.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Vishu Katha
* Krishna Mantra
* Vishnu Mantra
* Vishnu Sahasranama
* Krishna Aarti
* Vishnu Aarti
* Krishna Chalisa

faq.a1: Vishu is celebrated every year on 14 or 15 April.
faq.a2: Vishu marks the Malayalam New Year and celebrates prosperity, new beginnings, and divine blessings.
faq.a3: Vishu is primarily celebrated in Kerala and by Malayali communities around the world.
faq.a4: Vishukkani is the auspicious arrangement of sacred items viewed first on the morning of Vishu, believed to bring prosperity and good fortune throughout the year.
faq.a5: Vishu Kaineettam is the tradition in which elders give money or gifts to younger family members as a blessing for prosperity and success.
faq.q6: Which deity is worshipped during Vishu?
faq.a6: Lord Krishna (an incarnation of Lord Vishnu) is the principal deity worshipped during Vishu.
faq.q7: What are the main rituals of Vishu?
faq.a7: Viewing the Vishukkani, worshipping Lord Krishna, visiting temples, performing Aarti, preparing festive meals, and giving Vishu Kaineettam are the main rituals.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Vishu?
faq.a8: Vishu symbolizes new beginnings, prosperity, gratitude, family unity, hope, and the blessings of Lord Krishna for a peaceful and successful year.
